directions.name: restore accepted base, then narrow locality window
directions.hypothesis: Restore the accepted grouped_rows=8 + reversed row-pair + right-left PTX sweep + one-sync handoff base, then test whether reducing only kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 8 to 4 improves locality on the accepted consumer path without reopening the rejected grouped_rows=16 path.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band consumer-path locality and reuse window width on the accepted PTX sweep / handoff path.
directions.risk: Medium: shrinking the group size may be too small and lose the locality benefit that the accepted base already captures, but the change stays narrowly scoped.
metrics_to_recheck: cp.async wait / CTA handoff stall behavior
shared-memory replay or load conflict signals
directions.stop_condition: Only accept if it beats 25.634208 ms and keeps the accepted sweep order and handoff behavior intact.

directions.name: accepted base, then narrow overlap recovery
directions.hypothesis: Restore the accepted base first, then try a very narrow overlap-recovery tweak only after the current refill-order branch is closed; the residual loss is more likely a handoff overlap issue than a layout problem.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Refill-order overlap around the one-sync handoff and staged consumer refill path.
directions.risk: Higher than dir_01: small overlap changes can perturb scheduling and re-open refill-order regressions.
directions.stop_condition: Only accept if it improves on the restored base without reopening the refill-order branch.

directions.name: final consumer-order closure
directions.hypothesis: If the accepted right-left sweep remains the active base, the remaining opportunity is a final very local consumer-order closure after explicitly closing both the split sweep and the full mirrored sweep variants.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual consumer-order inefficiency in the hot-band PTX consumer path rather than grouping or refill layout.
directions.risk: Moderate: this is the narrowest remaining surface and easy to overfit if the accepted right-left sweep is still the right base.
metrics_to_recheck: shared-load replay and dependency stall signals
directions.stop_condition: Only pursue if the accepted right-left sweep stays active and the change does not replace it.

notes: Anchored to run 20260420_090210_bf16_gemm_v1_06ebe93 at 25.634208 ms. Rejected this round: grouped_rows=16, warmup-order reopen, K32 cadence, extra-live B lookahead, unroll-1 base, CTA-level B repack, broad shared-memory rewrites, and consumer-order variants that replace the accepted right-left sweep as the active base.
